title: Analytic Knowledge, Snkhya, Summarized
summary: Krishna teaches Uddhava about witness consciousness, detachment from family/objects, and devotion (Bhakti). Addresses relationship endings, dealing with grief, letting go of attachments, and mental calm.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “The Supreme Lord said: 'I shall now discuss with you the analytic knowledge as established by the classical authorities. Knowing this a person can immediately give up the bewilderment based…” and develops toward “For as long as I look after it [or cast My glance], the grand creation will perpetually, for the sake of the variegation of its qualities, generation…”

This passage begins with “When the form of the universe that is pervaded by Me has manifested the planetary variety of its time periods [of creation, maintenance and decay], this variety with its different worlds…” and develops toward “This is what I, the Supervisor of the Spiritual and Material world, had to say concerning this Snkhya instruction of analysis [see also 3.25 - 3.33]…”

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच अथ ते सम्प्रवक्ष्यामि साङ्ख्यं पूर्वैर् विनिश्चितम् । यद् विज्ञाय पुमान् सद्यो जह्याद् वैकल्पिकं भ्रमम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca atha te sampravakṣyāmi sāṅkhyaṃ pūrvair viniścitam / yad vijñāya pumān sadyo jahyād vaikalpikaṃ bhramam
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'I shall now discuss with you the analytic knowledge as established by the classical authorities. Knowing this a person can immediately give up the bewilderment based upon the material duality.

verses.sanskrit: आसीज् ज्ञानम् अथो अर्थ एकम् एवाविकल्पितम् । यदा विवेक-निपुणा आदौ कृत-युगे ऽयुगे
verses.transliteration: āsīj jñānam atho artha ekam evāvikalpitam / yadā viveka-nipuṇā ādau kṛta-yuge 'yuge
verses.translation: In the first age of dutifulness [Krita], in the beginning when there were [only] persons expert in [spiritual] discrimination, as also before that time [during the period of annihilation], the Knower was simply one and the same as the [universe] known [see also 11.22: 29].

verses.sanskrit: तन् माया-फल-रूपेण केवलं निर्विकल्पितम् । वाङ्-मनो-ऽगोचरं सत्यं द्विधा समभवद् बृहत्
verses.transliteration: tan māyā-phala-rūpeṇa kevalaṃ nirvikalpitam / vāṅ-mano-'gocaraṃ satyaṃ dvidhā samabhavad bṛhat
verses.translation: That One Great Undifferentiated Truth inaccessible to speech and mind [Brahman], turned into the two forms of material nature on the one hand and the enjoyment [of the enjoyer of that result] on the other hand [see 11.22].

verses.sanskrit: तयोर् एकतरो ह्य् अर्थः प्रकृतिः सोभयात्मिका । ज्ञानं त्व् अन्यतमो भावः पुरुषः सो ऽभिधीयते
verses.transliteration: tayor ekataro hy arthaḥ prakṛtiḥ sobhayātmikā / jñānaṃ tv anyatamo bhāvaḥ puruṣaḥ so 'bhidhīyate
verses.translation: One essential half, material nature [prakriti], is she [the 'mother'] who is of a dualistic nature, while he, the other entity, the knower, is called the purusha [the enjoying person or male principle].

verses.sanskrit: तमो रजः सत्त्वम् इति प्रकृतेर् अभवन् गुणाः । मया प्रक्षोभ्यमाणायाः पुरुषानुमतेन च
verses.transliteration: tamo rajaḥ sattvam iti prakṛter abhavan guṇāḥ / mayā prakṣobhyamāṇāyāḥ puruṣānumatena ca
verses.translation: By My agitation of the elementary nature [in the form of time, of kla], the modes of tamas, rajas and sattva [the gunas] have manifested in order to fulfill the desires of the living entity.

verses.sanskrit: तेभ्यः समभवत् सूत्रं महान् सूत्रेण संयुतः । ततो विकुर्वतो जातो यो ऽहङ्कारो विमोहनः
verses.transliteration: tebhyaḥ samabhavat sūtraṃ mahān sūtreṇa saṃyutaḥ / tato vikurvato jāto yo 'haṅkāro vimohanaḥ
verses.translation: From them the thread [the activating principle of the stra] arose, together with the principle of intelligence [mahat]. From the transformation of mahat came about the false ego [the ahankra of the purusha who identifies with the object of perception] that is the cause of bewilderment.

verses.sanskrit: वैकारिकस् तैजसश् च तामसश् चेत्य् अहं त्रि-वृत् । तन्-मात्रेन्द्रिय-मनसां कारणं चिद्-अचिन्-मयः
verses.transliteration: vaikārikas taijasaś ca tāmasaś cety ahaṃ tri-vṛt / tan-mātrendriya-manasāṃ kāraṇaṃ cid-acin-mayaḥ
verses.translation: That I-awareness is thus of the three [guna] categories and [accordingly makes] with clarity, emotion and ignorance [alternately use of] the sense objects [tanmtra], the senses [indriyas] and the mind [manas]. Thus it [the identified self] constitutes the cause of understanding and not understanding [the so-called conscious and unconscious].

verses.sanskrit: अर्थस् तन्-मात्रिकाज् जज्ञे तामसाद् इन्द्रियाणि च । तैजसाद् देवता आसन्न् एकादश च वैकृतात्
verses.transliteration: arthas tan-mātrikāj jajñe tāmasād indriyāṇi ca / taijasād devatā āsann ekādaśa ca vaikṛtāt
verses.translation: The darkness of the false ego gave rise to the subtle sensations [or perceptions] of gross matter, its emotion awakened the senses and the clarity of the identified self called for the eleven gods [see deva].

verses.sanskrit: मया सञ्चोदिता भावाः सर्वे संहत्य-कारिणः । अण्डम् उत्पादयाम् आसुर् ममायतनम् उत्तमम्
verses.transliteration: mayā sañcoditā bhāvāḥ sarve saṃhatya-kāriṇaḥ / aṇḍam utpādayām āsur mamāyatanam uttamam
verses.translation: Because all the elements combined to function under My influence, they brought about the egg of the universe that serves as My supreme residence [see from 11.22: 18].

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन्न् अहं समभवम् अण्डे सलिल-संस्थितौ । मम नाभ्याम् अभूत् पद्मं विश्वाख्यं तत्र चात्म-भूः
verses.transliteration: tasminn ahaṃ samabhavam aṇḍe salila-saṃsthitau / mama nābhyām abhūt padmaṃ viśvākhyaṃ tatra cātma-bhūḥ
verses.translation: I appeared [as Nryana] in the egg that was situated in the water of the causal ocean, and from My navel a lotus arose that is known as the universe. On that lotus the self-born one found his existence [Brahm, see 3.8].

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽसृजत् तपसा युक्तो रजसा मद्-अनुग्रहात् । लोकान् स-पालान् विश्वात्मा भूर् भुवः स्वर् इति त्रिधा
verses.transliteration: so 'sṛjat tapasā yukto rajasā mad-anugrahāt / lokān sa-pālān viśvātmā bhūr bhuvaḥ svar iti tridhā
verses.translation: He, the soul of the universe endowed with passion, created from his penance by My mercy the three different worlds called earth, the atmosphere [or ether] and heaven [bhh, bhuvah and svaha], as also its rulers [see Gyatr and loka].

verses.sanskrit: देवानाम् ओक आसीत् स्वर् भूतानां च भुवः पदम् । मर्त्यादीनां च भूर् लोकः सिद्धानां त्रितयात् परम्
verses.transliteration: devānām oka āsīt svar bhūtānāṃ ca bhuvaḥ padam / martyādīnāṃ ca bhūr lokaḥ siddhānāṃ tritayāt param
verses.translation: Heaven became the residence of the demigods, the atmosphere the home of the ghostly spirits, the earthly places offered the humans and other living beings shelter and the place beyond these three is there for the Siddhas, the souls of perfection [Siddhaloka].

verses.sanskrit: अधो ऽसुराणां नागानां भूमेर् ओको ऽसृजत् प्रभुः । त्रि-लोक्यां गतयः सर्वाः कर्मणां त्रि-गुणात्मनाम्
verses.transliteration: adho 'surāṇāṃ nāgānāṃ bhūmer oko 'sṛjat prabhuḥ / tri-lokyāṃ gatayaḥ sarvāḥ karmaṇāṃ tri-guṇātmanām
verses.translation: The places of the underworld were by the master created as the residence for all asuras ['unenlightened souls' or demons] and those perfect in their ego [the 'snakes', the Ngas]. All the destinations of the three worlds thus owe their existence to the fruitful activities proper to the modes [see B.G. 4: 17, 10.1: 42-43].

verses.sanskrit: योगस्य तपसश् चैव न्यासस्य गतयो ऽमलाः । महर् जनस् तपः सत्यं भक्ति-योगस्य मद्-गतिः
verses.transliteration: yogasya tapasaś caiva nyāsasya gatayo 'malāḥ / mahar janas tapaḥ satyaṃ bhakti-yogasya mad-gatiḥ
verses.translation: By penance, yoga and by forsaking [in sannysa] one is of the spotless destinations of mahar, janas, tapas and satya, but My destination [Vaikunthha] is reached by performing devotional service.

verses.sanskrit: मया कालात्मना धात्रा कर्म-युक्तम् इदं जगत् । गुण-प्रवाह एतस्मिन्न् उन्मज्जति निमज्जति
verses.transliteration: mayā kālātmanā dhātrā karma-yuktam idaṃ jagat / guṇa-pravāha etasminn unmajjati nimajjati
verses.translation: As arranged by Me, the Supporter, the Soul [the energy] of Time, one rises up from or drowns in the mighty stream of the modes of this world in which one is bound to performing fruitive labor.

verses.sanskrit: अणुर् बृहत् कृशः स्थूलो यो यो भावः प्रसिध्यति । सर्वो ऽप्य् उभय-संयुक्तः प्रकृत्या पुरुषेण च
verses.transliteration: aṇur bṛhat kṛśaḥ sthūlo yo yo bhāvaḥ prasidhyati / sarvo 'py ubhaya-saṃyuktaḥ prakṛtyā puruṣeṇa ca
verses.translation: Whatever the small, the big, the thin and the massive of manifestation, is all brought about by the combination of material nature and its enjoyer [see also B.G. 18: 16].

verses.sanskrit: यस् तु यस्यादिर् अन्तश् च स वै मध्यं च तस्य सन् । विकारो व्यवहारार्थो यथा तैजस-पार्थिवाः
verses.transliteration: yas tu yasyādir antaś ca sa vai madhyaṃ ca tasya san / vikāro vyavahārārtho yathā taijasa-pārthivāḥ
verses.translation: That what constitutes the cause of something - common matters like things of gold and things of clay - is there in the beginning, during the life as also in the end of that what was produced and is subject to transformation [and is thus illusory as for its form, compare 6.16: 22, 10.87: 15, 11.22: 8].

verses.sanskrit: यद् उपादाय पूर्वस् तु भावो विकुरुते ऽपरम् । आदिर् अन्तो यदा यस्य तत् सत्यम् अभिधीयते
verses.transliteration: yad upādāya pūrvas tu bhāvo vikurute 'param / ādir anto yadā yasya tat satyam abhidhīyate
verses.translation: Something that serves as a previous ingredient of a thing that - as something different - constitutes a change of form of that ingredient, is called the true of something provided it is present from the beginning to the end [compare B.G. 2: 13, 2: 16].

verses.sanskrit: प्रकृतिर् यस्योपादानम् आधारः पुरुषः परः । सतो ऽभिव्यञ्जकः कालो ब्रह्म तत् त्रितयं त्व् अहम्
verses.transliteration: prakṛtir yasyopādānam ādhāraḥ puruṣaḥ paraḥ / sato 'bhivyañjakaḥ kālo brahma tat tritayaṃ tv aham
verses.translation: Material nature [prakriti] the foundation of which is constituted by the causal [transformed] ingredient of the Supreme Person [the purusha], together with that what is the agitating agent, viz. Time [kla], makes up the threefold of the Absolute Truth [Brahman] that I am.

verses.sanskrit: सर्गः प्रवर्तते तावत् पौर्वापर्येण नित्यशः । महान् गुण-विसर्गार्थः स्थित्य्-अन्तो यावद् ईक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: sargaḥ pravartate tāvat paurvāparyeṇa nityaśaḥ / mahān guṇa-visargārthaḥ sthity-anto yāvad īkṣaṇam
verses.translation: For as long as I look after it [or cast My glance], the grand creation will perpetually, for the sake of the variegation of its qualities, generation after generation continue to exist until its dissolution [see also B.G. 3: 24].

verses.sanskrit: विराण् मयासाद्यमानो लोक-कल्प-विकल्पकः । पञ्चत्वाय विशेषाय कल्पते भुवनैः सह
verses.transliteration: virāṇ mayāsādyamāno loka-kalpa-vikalpakaḥ / pañcatvāya viśeṣāya kalpate bhuvanaiḥ saha
verses.translation: When the form of the universe that is pervaded by Me has manifested the planetary variety of its time periods [of creation, maintenance and decay], this variety with its different worlds [losing its synergy] arrives at [a dissolution into] its five composing gross elements [see yugas, manvantaras, and B.G. 11: 13].

verses.sanskrit: अन्ने प्रलीयते मर्त्यम् अन्नं धानासु लीयते । धाना भूमौ प्रलीयन्ते भूमिर् गन्धे प्रलीयते
verses.transliteration: anne pralīyate martyam annaṃ dhānāsu līyate / dhānā bhūmau pralīyante bhūmir gandhe pralīyate
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: अप्सु प्रलीयते गन्ध आपश् च स्व-गुणे रसे । लीयते ज्योतिषि रसो ज्योती रूपे प्रलीयते
verses.transliteration: apsu pralīyate gandha āpaś ca sva-guṇe rase / līyate jyotiṣi raso jyotī rūpe pralīyate
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: रूपं वायौ स च स्पर्शे लीयते सो ऽपि चाम्बरे । अम्बरं शब्द-तन्-मात्र इन्द्रियाणि स्व-योनिषु
verses.transliteration: rūpaṃ vāyau sa ca sparśe līyate so 'pi cāmbare / ambaraṃ śabda-tan-mātra indriyāṇi sva-yoniṣu
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: योनिर् वैकारिके सौम्य लीयते मनसीश्वरे । शब्दो भूतादिम् अप्येति भूतादिर् महति प्रभुः
verses.transliteration: yonir vaikārike saumya līyate manasīśvare / śabdo bhūtādim apyeti bhūtādir mahati prabhuḥ
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: स लीयते महान् स्वेषु गुणेसु गुण-वत्तमः । ते ऽव्यक्ते सम्प्रलीयन्ते तत् काले लीयते ऽव्यये
verses.transliteration: sa līyate mahān sveṣu guṇesu guṇa-vattamaḥ / te 'vyakte sampralīyante tat kāle līyate 'vyaye
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: कालो माया-मये जीवे जीव आत्मनि मय्य् अजे । आत्मा केवल आत्म-स्थो विकल्पापाय-लक्षणः
verses.transliteration: kālo māyā-maye jīve jīva ātmani mayy aje / ātmā kevala ātma-stho vikalpāpāya-lakṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: The mortal frame [at the time of annihilation] will merge with the food, the food with the grains, the grains with the earth and the earth with the fragrance. Fragrance becomes merged with the water, the water with its quality of taste, the taste with the fire and the fire with the form. Form merges with air, air merges with touch and touch merges thereupon with the ether. Ether merges with the subtle object of sound and the senses [of sound etc.] become merged with their sources [the gods of the sun and moon etc.]. The sources, My dear Uddhava, merge with the mind of the ego of goodness, the controller of the sound, that dissolves in the original state of the elements [the ego of slowness]. This all-powerful primal elementary nature then merges with the cosmic intelligence [mahat]. That greater principle dissolves in its own basic qualities and they in their turn merge with their ultimate abode, the unmanifest state of nature that merges with the infallible Time. Time merges with the individuality [the jva] of the Supreme in command of the illusory potency and that individuality merges with Me, the Supreme Self Unborn [tm], who, characterized by creation and annihilation, is perfectly established in Himself and remains alone [see also 3.11: 28, 4.23: 15-18, 11.3: 12-15].

verses.sanskrit: एवम् अन्वीक्षमाणस्य कथं वैकल्पिको भ्रमः । मनसो हृदि तिष्ठेत व्योम्नीवार्कोदये तमः
verses.transliteration: evam anvīkṣamāṇasya kathaṃ vaikalpiko bhramaḥ / manaso hṛdi tiṣṭheta vyomnīvārkodaye tamaḥ
verses.translation: Just as with the darkness when the sun rises in the sky, how can the bewilderment of the dual mind remain in the heart of the one who seriously studies this?

verses.sanskrit: एष साङ्ख्य-विधिः प्रोक्तः संशय-ग्रन्थि-भेदनः । प्रतिलोमानुलोमाभ्यां परावर-दृश मया
verses.transliteration: eṣa sāṅkhya-vidhiḥ proktaḥ saṃśaya-granthi-bhedanaḥ / pratilomānulomābhyāṃ parāvara-dṛśa mayā
verses.translation: This is what I, the Supervisor of the Spiritual and Material world, had to say concerning this Snkhya instruction of analysis [see also 3.25 - 3.33] that breaks through the bondage of doubts of the people who both go along with and go against nature.'
